mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.5% in a single monthly release
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and consumer credit availability simultaneously signals a demand-side liquidity crunch. This dual compression of real output and financial leverage forces equity risk premiums higher, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated policy rates, an acceleration in industrial production acts as a supply-side inflation catalyst. This forces the market to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which disproportionately penalizes growth equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% for three consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when policy rates fall below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in new housing starts reflects rising consumer confidence and credit access, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST consistently fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, it indicates robust labor demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn favorable.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS exceeds 200000 for two consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Consecutive payrolls above 200,000 fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's win rate does not exceed 55% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real GDP and stable consumer price indices signals a healthy, non-inflationary growth expansion. This optimal macro backdrop reduces discount rate uncertainty while boosting cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint observation of rising GDPC1 and stable CPIAUCSL does not yield positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for equity risk premium proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production coupled with a rising unemployment rate signals a supply-demand mismatch that typically precedes corporate margin compression. This dual deterioration triggers negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants price in near-term earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines >1.5% month-over-month while UNRATE rises >0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: The simultaneous drop in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below chance over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
